extractedText:
Sample Project Document: HVAC System Upgrade
Project ID: SR-2026-HVAC-01
Status: In Progress
Project Manager: Abhinav Jain
Execu(cid:415)ve Summary
This project involves the transi(cid:415)on from tradi(cid:415)onal vapor compression units to a high-efficiency VRF
(Variable Refrigerant Flow) system at the North Campus facility. The primary goal is to reduce energy
consump(cid:415)on by $25\%$ by op(cid:415)mizing the power factor and improving insula(cid:415)on quality across all
zones. We are also integra(cid:415)ng so(cid:332) starters for all new compressors to mi(cid:415)gate inrush current spikes
that have previously caused voltage fluctua(cid:415)ons in the local grid.
Technical Specifica(cid:415)ons
 Opera(cid:415)ng Voltage: 415V (High voltage transmission to be maintained for efficiency).
 Refrigerant: R-134a (Eco-friendly transi(cid:415)on).
 Motor Type: 3-Phase Induc(cid:415)on Motors with laminated stator cores to minimize eddy current
losses.
Ac(cid:415)on Items & Task Extrac(cid:415)on
The following tasks must be completed to stay within the fiscal quarter's budget:
1. System Audit: Perform a full audit of current eddy current losses in the exis(cid:415)ng transformer
bank.
o Deadline: March 30, 2026
2. Procurement: Order the ammonia-water absorbent pairs for the secondary absorp(cid:415)on
cooling unit.
o Deadline: April 05, 2026
3. Lamina(cid:415)on Check: Ensure all new transformer cores are properly laminated before
installa(cid:415)on.
o Deadline: March 12, 2026
4. Contract Review: Re-nego(cid:415)ate the Contract Demand with the u(cid:415)lity provider to ensure it
aligns with our new maximum demand projec(cid:415)ons.
o Deadline: January 01, 2026
Risk Management
Failure to meet the insula(cid:415)on quality standards will result in a $15\%$ increase in projected energy
costs. Monitoring of the evaporator stages is required to ensure refrigerant expansion is occurring at
op(cid:415)mal pressure.

zones. We are also integra(cid:415)ng so(cid:332) starters for all new compressors to mi(cid:415)gate inrush current spikes
that have previously caused voltage fluctua(cid:415)ons in the local grid.
Technical Specifica(cid:415)ons
 Opera(cid:415)ng Voltage: 415V (High voltage transmission to be maintained for efficiency).
 Refrigerant: R-134a (Eco-friendly transi(cid:415)on).
 Motor Type: 3-Phase Induc(cid:415)on Motors with laminated stator cores to minimize eddy current
losses.
Ac(cid:415)on Items & Task Extrac(cid:415)on
The following tasks must be completed to stay within the fiscal quarter's budget:
1. System Audit: Perform a full audit of current eddy current losses in the exis(cid:415)ng transformer
bank.
o Deadline: April 10, 2026
2. Procurement: Order the ammonia-water absorbent pairs for the secondary absorp(cid:415)on
cooling unit.
o Deadline: Ap(cid:396)il 15, 2026
3. Lamina(cid:415)on Check: Ensure all new transformer cores are properly laminated before
installa(cid:415)on.
o Deadline: April 25, 2026
4. Contract Review: Re-nego(cid:415)ate the Contract Demand with the u(cid:415)lity provider to ensure it
aligns with our new maximum demand projec(cid:415)ons.
o Deadline: March 29, 2026
Risk Management
Failure to meet the insula(cid:415)on quality standards will result in a $15\%$ increase in projected energy
costs. Monitoring of the evaporator stages is required to ensure refrigerant expansion is occurring at
op(cid:415)mal pressure.
